title: 5'RACE (Rapid Amplification of cDNA ends) Protocol
author: Anna Behle
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jk7ckzn

steps.title: Prepare Bacterial Culture and Extract RNA
steps.instruction: Prepare bacterial culture using culture conditions of choice (e.g. high salt, oxidative stress, etc.) Harvest cells and extract total RNA. Isolation of total RNA from Synechocystis (PGTX method) by Anna Behle.

steps.title: DNase I Treatment
steps.instruction: Mix 30 µg total RNA with 4 µL 10x DNase I buffer, 3 µL DNase I, 1 µL RNase Inhibitor (e.g. RiboLock, ThermoScientific), and H2O ad 40 µL. Incubate 30 min at 37˚C. Use TURBO DNA-free™ Kit (Thermo Scientific Catalog #AM1907).

steps.title: RNA Cleanup with Roti Aqua P/C/I
steps.instruction: Add 1 vol. Roti Aqua P/C/I. Mix well, then centrifuge at 4˚C for 10 min at maximum speed. Transfer to a fresh tube. (Roti Aqua P/C/I Carl Roth Catalog #X985.1)

steps.title: RNA Precipitation (Overnight)
steps.instruction: Add 1/10 vol 3M NaOAc, pH=5.3, + 3 vol 100% EtOH. Precipitate RNA at -20˚C over night. Centrifuge 30 min at maximum speed and 4˚C. Discard supernatant. Wash with 500 µL 70% EtOH. Centrifuge 15 min, 4˚C at maximum speed. Completely remove supernatant. Dry pellet for 5 min. Resuspend RNA in 60 µL RNase-free water.

steps.title: RppH Treatment
steps.instruction: Prepare reaction for RppH treatment: 5µg DNase-treated RNA, 10 µL 10x NEB 2 buffer, 5 µL RppH, 1 µL RNase Inhibitor, H2O ad 100 µL. Incubate for 1 h at 37˚C. RppH removes pyrophosphate from triphosphorylated ends of mRNA. (RNA 5’ Pyrophosphohydrolase (RppH) - New England Biolabs Catalog #M0356S)

steps.title: Cleanup and RNA Precipitation
steps.instruction: Inactivate enzymes: Add 1 vol. Roti Aqua P/C/I. Mix well, then centrifuge at 4˚C for 10 min at maximum speed. Transfer to a fresh tube. Add 1/10 vol 3M NaOAc, pH=5.3, + 3 vol 100% EtOH + 1 µL glycogen (ThermoScientific, RNA-grade). Precipitate RNA at -20˚C over night. Centrifuge 30 min at maximum speed and 4˚C. Discard supernatant. Wash with 500 µL 70% EtOH. Centrifuge 15 min, 4˚C at maximum speed. Completely remove supernatant. Dry pellet for 5 min. Resuspend RNA in 67 µL (for samples 1, 3) or 134 µL (for sample 2) RNase-free water.

steps.title: RNA Adapter Ligation
steps.instruction: Perform 5´-RNA-oligo linker ligation. T4 RNA Ligase 1 catalyzes the ligation of 5' phosphorylated ssRNA to 3' OH. The 5'-end of RNA oligo should not be phosphorylated to prevent undesired ligation to mRNA 3' end. Incubate 1 hour at 37˚C (or, alternatively, 12 h at 17˚C). (T4 RNA Ligase 1 (ssRNA Ligase) - New England Biolabs Catalog #M0204S)

steps.title: Cleanup and RNA Precipitation (Post-Ligation)
steps.instruction: Inactivate enzymes: Add 1 vol. Roti Aqua P/C/I. Mix well, then centrifuge at 4˚C for 10 min at maximum speed. Transfer to a fresh tube. Add 1/10 vol 3M NaOAc, pH=5.3, + 3 vol 100% EtOH + 1 µL glycogen (ThermoScientific, RNA-grade). Precipitate RNA at -20˚C over night, or at least 1 h at -80˚C. Centrifuge 30 min at maximum speed and 4˚C. Discard supernatant. Wash with 500 µL 70% EtOH. Centrifuge 15 min, 4˚C at maximum speed. Completely remove supernatant. Dry pellet for 5 min. Resuspend in 20 µL RNase-free water.

steps.title: Reverse Transcription
steps.instruction: Combine 5 µL linked RNA (add H2O up to 32.5 µL) and 2 pmol gene specific primer 1 (0.2 µL of 10µM stock). Denature at 95˚C for 5 min, then chill on ice. Add 10 µL 5x First strand buffer, 1 µL 10 µM dNTPs, 1 µL RNase Inhibitor (e.g. RiboLock, ThermoScientific), 2.5 µL 0.1 M DTT, and 2 µL Superscript III. Incubate 1 hour at 55 ˚C. (SuperScript® III First-Strand Synthesis System Thermo Scientific Catalog #18080-051)

steps.title: Cleanup and cDNA Precipitation
steps.instruction: Add 1 vol. Roti Aqua P/C/I. Mix well, then centrifuge at 4˚C for 10 min at maximum speed. Transfer to a fresh tube. Add 1/10 vol 3M NaOAc, pH=5.3, + 3 vol 100% EtOH + 1 µL glycogen (ThermoScientific, RNA-grade). Precipitate RNA at -20˚C over night, or at least 1 h at -80˚C. Centrifuge 30 min at maximum speed and 4˚C. Discard supernatant. Wash with 500 µL 70% EtOH. Centrifuge 15 min, 4˚C at maximum speed. Completely remove supernatant. Dry pellet for 5 min. Resuspend cDNA in 21 µL RNase-free water.

steps.title: RNase H Treatment
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL RNase H; incubate at 37˚C for 20 min. RNase H specifically removes RNA from DNA:RNA hybrid molecules, but not ss- or dsDNA. (RNase H - New England Biolabs Catalog #M0297S)

steps.title: Perform PCR with Nested Primers
steps.instruction: Perform a PCR with Adapter-specific primer and one nested gene specific primer. Use 5 µL 5x Q5 rxn buffer, 5 µL 5x High GC buffer, 0.5 µL Adapter-Primer 1, 0.5 µL Gene specific primer (nested), 0.5 µL dNTPs, 4 µL cDNA, 0.5 µL Q5 Polymerase, H2O ad 25 µL. Cycling conditions: 98˚C 2 min; then 30-35 cycles of 98˚C 10 sec | xx˚C 5 sec | 72˚C 10 sec; followed by 72˚C 5 min. Make sure to include a control PCR on DNase-treated RNA to ensure there is no genomic contamination.

steps.title: Agarose Gel Electrophoresis and Band Excision
steps.instruction: Run PCR products on a 1.8% agarose gel for approx. 1.5 h, or, in case of very small fragments, a 3% NuSieve Agarose gel. If an RppH+-specific band is observed: Excise and gel-extract band with the highest molecular weight. If multiple +RppH specific bands are present, all should be analyzed.

steps.title: Subcloning into a Cloning Vector
steps.instruction: Subclone RACE-PCR fragment into cloning vector of choice, e.g. pJET or TOPO. Transform DH5α with ligation mix.

steps.title: Colony PCR and Sequencing
steps.instruction: Perform colony PCR on colonies from transformation to ensure successful ligation. In the case of positive clones, purify the plasmids and sequence them. At least five, possibly more, should be analyzed.
